Title: Starch Synthase 3 isoforms are essential for normal starch granule initiation in wheat endosperm

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Kronos for bgc1-3 comparison; bread wheat sequences used in interaction assays
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: granule initiation protein; PTST2/FLO6/BGC1 family-associated factor
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a granule initiation component interacting with SS3a and SS3b; bgc1 mutant causes supernumerary initiation and mainly compound granules, providing mechanistic context for SS3-dependent semicompound granule formation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation with SS3a-N in Nicotiana benthamiana; mutant phenotype comparison using bgc1-3
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BGC1 interacts with both SS3a and SS3b. 2. BGC1 is a known component of starch granule initiation. 3. bgc1 mutant comparison helped show that SS3 mutants and bgc1 both generate supernumerary initiations, but differ in whether granules become semicompound or fully compound.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for engineering starch granule initiation patterns, but this paper mainly supports its use as an interacting granule-initiation component rather than as the focal engineering target
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it was directly tested in Results through interaction assays and mutant comparison, but it was not the principal genetic subject of the study

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: durum wheat / heterologous yeast assay context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with SS3a but not SS3b, suggesting isoform-specific linkage between SS3a and granule initiation machinery
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MRC interacted with SS3a in yeast two-hybrid assays. 2. No interaction with SS3b was detected. 3. This suggests SS3a may have a more specific connection to granule initiation timing machinery than SS3b.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential future combinatorial target for modifying granule initiation timing, but not functionally dissected here
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly assayed in Results but not a primary focal gene; discussed as a known controller of B-type granule initiation timing

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary starch fraction with reduced digestibility; desirable for improving human health-related grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: amylopectin synthesis and starch granule initiation in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glycosyltransferase starch synthase family; C-terminal glucosyltransferase domain; carbohydrate-binding modules (CBMs); plastid transit peptide; SS3a-specific extended N-terminal region
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate alpha-1,4-glucan chains during starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream polymer elongation step linked to granule initiation control
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for normal starch polymer biosynthesis and granule initiation; loss increases resistant starch
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: amyloplast/chloroplast stroma-associated starch granule biosynthetic compartment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In bread wheat, transcripts are mainly detected in spike and grains and are almost undetectable in roots, stems and leaves. In durum wheat developing endosperm, transcripts are present from 6-30 dpa and peak between 10 and 20 dpa
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm during grain filling under climate-controlled glasshouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal A-type starch granule initiation and morphology while limiting resistant starch accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for normal A-type starch granule initiation and important determinant of resistant starch, but rate-limiting status not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING knockout mutants and genetic crosses; granule morphology by SEM and polarized light microscopy; Coulter counter granule sizing; starch, amylose and resistant starch quantification; native PAGE activity staining; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS3a is the dominant SS3 isoform in wheat endosperm and is highly expressed during grain filling. 2. ss3a mutants form lobed semicompound A-type granules due to multiple initiation points per amyloplast. 3. Loss of SS3a reduces A-type granule size and increases relative B-type granule volume. 4. ss3a elevates amylose and resistant starch. 5. SS3a interacts with itself, SS3b, BGC1 and MRC, linking starch polymer synthesis with granule initiation machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious plant growth, grain appearance, TGW, or grain size penalties under controlled conditions; total starch may be slightly reduced
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for improving resistant starch and modifying granule morphology in wheat; can be combined with SS3b knockout to further boost resistant starch while retaining near-normal grain weight
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defective granules are semicompound rather than fully compound; phenotype appears early at 8 dpa and primarily affects A-type granules. TtSS3a-B1 may be the dominant homeolog based on immunoblot band intensity. Bread wheat orthologs are TraesCS1A02G091500; TraesCS1B02G119300; TraesCS1D02G100100
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from Ma et al. (2021) for bread wheat organ expression; RNA-seq from Chen et al. (2023) for durum wheat developing endosperm; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary starch fraction with reduced digestibility; desirable for improving human health-related grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: amylopectin synthesis and starch granule initiation in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: glycosyltransferase starch synthase family; C-terminal glucosyltransferase domain; carbohydrate-binding modules (CBMs); plastid transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate alpha-1,4-glucan chains during starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream polymer elongation step with partial overlap in granule initiation and polymer biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: partially supports normal starch polymer biosynthesis and granule initiation; loss alone has little effect but enhances resistant starch phenotype with ss3a loss
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: amyloplast/chloroplast stroma-associated starch granule biosynthetic compartment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In bread wheat, transcripts are detected in all tissues examined. In durum wheat developing endosperm, transcripts are present from 6-30 dpa at around 10-fold lower levels than SS3a and are relatively constant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly expressed; present in developing endosperm at low levels across 6-30 dpa
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm during grain filling under climate-controlled glasshouse conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SS3b alone has no detectable effect on granule morphology, starch content, amylose, or resistant starch, but combined loss with SS3a exacerbates semicompound granule formation, decreases A- and B-type granule size, and further increases amylose and resistant starch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting alone; partial compensatory contributor to SS3a-dependent control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING knockout mutants and genetic crosses; SEM and polarized light microscopy; Coulter counter granule sizing; starch, amylose and resistant starch quantification; native PAGE activity staining; yeast two-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS3b is expressed in wheat endosperm but at much lower levels than SS3a. 2. ss3b single mutants show no clear endosperm starch phenotype. 3. Removing SS3b in an ss3a background strongly enhances semicompound granule formation and resistant starch accumulation. 4. SS3b interacts with itself, SS3a and BGC1, supporting overlapping function with SS3a.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalties in plant growth, grain appearance, TGW, grain size, starch content, amylose or resistant starch when mutated alone; in double mutant, total starch may be slightly reduced
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking target with SS3a to achieve stronger resistant starch increase than SS3a knockout alone
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bread wheat orthologs are TraesCS2A02G468800; TraesCS2B02G491700; TraesCS2D02G468900. Native PAGE suggests participation with SS3a in a putative heteromeric complex
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from Ma et al. (2021) for bread wheat organ expression; RNA-seq from Chen et al. (2023) for durum wheat developing endosperm; no accession reported
